skill.name: geo-content-brief-skill
skill.description: Turns Chinese GEO interviews, keyword notes, competitor signals, and channel constraints into actionable content briefs for writers and editors.
skill.summary: Create structured Chinese GEO content briefs from research notes, keywords, and channel constraints.
skill.author: yaojingang

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-severity hits, but review found they are mostly false positives from Chinese text, Markdown backticks, generated reports, and static HTML. No prompt injection, automatic network request, credential access, or executable payload was found in the reviewed core skill files. Minor publication risk remains because generated report pages contain command examples and parent-directory links that should be treated as documentation, not trusted runtime behavior.

medium_findings.title: Generated Report Artifacts Contain Command Examples
medium_findings.description: The generated review studio includes shell command examples such as Python validation commands. They are displayed as documentation in static HTML, not automatically executed, but marketplace users could copy them without verifying that the referenced scripts exist or are safe.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command text is explicit and user-visible, but it appears inside static HTML documentation. I did not find evidence that the skill executes these commands automatically.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives From Markdown and Report Text
low_findings.description: Most external command and weak cryptography findings point to Markdown backticks, file names, generated report language, or Chinese text. The reviewed core files describe a content brief workflow and do not contain Ruby backtick execution or weak cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are prose, YAML metadata, or reference text. Manual review found no cryptographic API calls, shell interpolation, or executable Ruby code at those locations.

low_findings.title: Static HTML Uses Parent Directory Links
low_findings.description: The generated review studio links to parent-directory files such as ../SKILL.md and ../security/permission_policy.md. This is not path traversal code, but it can expose or reference files outside the report directory when opened locally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory references are visible in static links and JSON metadata. They are navigation links rather than filesystem reads, so impact is limited.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: Targeted review for phrases that override analysis instructions, claim pre-approval, or tell reviewers to skip security checks found no evidence in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Searches for common prompt-injection language returned no matches. Confidence is high for reviewed files, but the package contains many generated report artifacts.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documentation-Only Command Strings
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis detected many command-like strings in Markdown and HTML report artifacts. These should remain documentation examples and should not be treated as trusted install or runtime commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is real because command strings are present, but the reviewed evidence shows static documentation rather than automatic execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local-Only HTML Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill-overview report contains a small inline script for language switching, scroll progress, and navigation state. Review found no fetch, sendBeacon, WebSocket, cookie access, eval, or dynamic Function use in the HTML reports.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script manipulates DOM state only. A targeted search found no network or dynamic code execution APIs in the HTML report files.

content.value_statement: Teams often receive scattered interviews, keywords, competitor notes, and channel limits that are hard to turn into writer-ready direction. This skill organizes those inputs into a focused Chinese GEO content brief with structure, evidence needs, risks, and next actions.
actual_capabilities: Converts Chinese interview notes, keyword lists, competitor summaries, and channel constraints into a one-page brief.
Separates target reader, search intent, content angle, structure, evidence needs, risk zones, and acceptance criteria.
Asks up to three clarification questions when core inputs are missing.
Uses trigger and output examples to distinguish briefs from full article writing or ad copy requests.
Flags missing evidence instead of inventing search volume, rankings, customer quotes, or citations.
Provides a review checklist for editors before handing the brief to writers.
limitations: It is written mainly for Simplified Chinese GEO and SEO content planning workflows.
It does not write complete long-form articles or replace brand strategy decisions.
It cannot verify search volume, rankings, screenshots, or third-party claims without supplied evidence.
Generated report artifacts include review guidance, but they are not proof of production runtime testing.

use_cases.title: Prepare Writer Handoffs
use_cases.description: Turn raw research, customer notes, and channel rules into a brief that writers can execute without guessing.

use_cases.title: Plan GEO Topic Angles
use_cases.description: Map keywords, intent, competitor signals, and evidence gaps into article angles and structure options.

use_cases.title: Review Brief Quality
use_cases.description: Check whether a planned brief includes audience, evidence, risk language, and next steps before drafting starts.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Brief From Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use geo-content-brief-skill. Turn these interview notes, keywords, channel constraints, and brand boundaries into a Chinese GEO content brief for a writer.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify Missing Inputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use geo-content-brief-skill. Review my partial keyword list and draft direction. Ask up to three clarification questions before creating the brief.

prompt_templates.title: Competitor-Informed Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Use geo-content-brief-skill. Compare these competitor page summaries with our product notes and create a brief with angle, structure, evidence needs, and risk warnings.

prompt_templates.title: Editorial Gate Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use geo-content-brief-skill. Audit this planned Chinese GEO brief against the review checklist. Identify missing evidence, weak structure, risky claims, and next actions.

output_examples.input: Customer interview notes, eight GEO keywords, a Zhihu channel limit, and a list of banned brand phrases.
output_examples.output: A one-page Chinese brief with input summary, target reader, search intent, recommended angle, section outline, evidence checklist, risk zone, and writer next steps.

output_examples.input: Only a keyword list and a rough product direction are available.
output_examples.output: A gap list, up to three clarification questions, and a marked draft brief that labels unverified assumptions as pending evidence.

output_examples.input: Competitor page summaries plus internal product notes for a comparison article.
output_examples.output: A brief that separates competitor signals from owned claims, proposes a differentiated angle, and lists proof needed before drafting.

best_practices: Provide interview notes, keywords, channel rules, audience details, and brand restrictions together when possible.
Mark which claims are verified and which facts still need evidence before drafting starts.
Use the review checklist before sending the brief to a writer or editor.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to write a complete long-form article when you need a planning brief.
Do not treat unsupported search volume, rankings, screenshots, or quotes as facts.
Do not use the generated report command examples as trusted runtime commands without review.

faq.question: Does this skill write full articles?
faq.answer: No. It creates a planning brief for writers and editors. It should not replace full drafting or brand strategy work.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Interview notes, keywords, competitor summaries, target channel, target reader, brand boundaries, and forbidden claims work best.

faq.question: Can it handle missing information?
faq.answer: Yes. It should ask up to three clarification questions and label missing evidence instead of inventing facts.

faq.question: Is it only for Chinese content?
faq.answer: The workflow and references are written for Chinese GEO content briefs. Other languages may need adaptation.

faq.question: Does it verify SEO data?
faq.answer: No. It organizes supplied data and highlights gaps. It does not independently verify search volume, rankings, or competitor claims.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the skill metadata and portable interface files.
